adds.rationale: Rocket Lab completed its 30th Electron launch on September 15, 2022 (its 7th of the year), delivered its 150th satellite to orbit, and simultaneously signed a DoD agreement to explore using Electron and Neutron vehicles for military cargo transport, with $24.35M Space Force funding secured for Neutron's upper stage — concrete commercial and defense milestones that position RKLB as the most liquid pure-play on the rockets/spacecraft wave named first in the profile's 'next waves' list.

news_evidence.summary: Rocket Lab successfully executed its 30th Electron launch 'The Owl Spreads Its Wings' on September 15, 2022, deploying Synspective's StriX-1 SAR satellite (mission 7 of 2022) and marking the 150th satellite delivered to orbit since 2017; the article also reports Rocket Lab signed a U.S. DoD agreement to explore using Neutron and Electron rockets for worldwide military cargo transport and on-orbit depot missions.

SatNews announced the upcoming 'Owl Spreads Its Wings' launch for Synspective's StriX-1 synthetic aperture radar Earth-observation satellite, part of a bulk buy of three Electron flights; the payload is part of a planned 30-satellite commercial radar imaging constellation.

adds.rationale: Russia's ongoing invasion of Ukraine has triggered a structural step-up in NATO defense budgets; the SPADE Defense Index fell only 3.9% through June 30, 2022 while the broader market fell 20.6%, and defense ETF assets were growing rapidly — making ITA the clearest liquid vehicle to express the profile's explicitly named geopolitical-rearmament wave in this bear market quarter.

news_evidence.summary: Defense News op-ed (August 8, 2022) documents that the SPADE Defense Index declined only 3.9% through June 30, 2022 versus the broader market down 20.6%; Invesco PPA aerospace/defense ETF assets more than doubled by June 2022; Ukraine-driven demand for Javelin and Stinger systems is creating sustained production backlogs and long-term revenue visibility for U.S. defense primes.

Nasdaq article reports U.S. defense stocks outpaced the broader S&P 500 after Russia intensified its Ukraine operations; Lockheed Martin and Northrop Grumman each gained over 15% from February 24, 2022, while the Dow Jones U.S. Aerospace & Defense Index rose 10% over the same period.

adds.rationale: BOTZ (Global X Robotics & AI ETF, launched 2016) provides diversified exposure to the second 'next wave' named in the profile — industrial robotics — at a moment when top holding Fanuc America announced a campus near-doubling on July 29, 2022 to meet surging automation demand driven by labor shortages and EV production ramp, consistent with the wave entering its commercial buildup phase.

news_evidence.summary: Fanuc America (a top BOTZ holding) announced on July 29, 2022 that it will nearly double its Michigan campus from roughly one million to two million square feet, citing 'tremendous demand for automation solutions' from EV manufacturing, warehousing, logistics, and food/beverage sectors as companies turn to robotics to solve persistent labor shortages.

The Global X Robotics & AI ETF (BOTZ) filed an updated 497K prospectus supplement with the SEC in 2022, confirming the fund's active status and its index covering companies in industrial robotics, non-industrial robots, and autonomous vehicles; top holdings include ABB, Keyence, Fanuc, NVDA, and Intuitive Surgical.

rationale_overall: The first quarterly rebalance fills three of the six wave buckets left uncovered by the Day-0 starter set (AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA/SPY, which saturate the AI bucket at its 4-ticker cap). RKLB adds the profile's top-priority 'next wave' (rockets/spacecraft) via the most liquid public launch provider, whose September 2022 milestone launches and DoD partnership confirm commercial and defense traction. ITA captures the profile's geopolitical-rearmament wave — the most structurally active non-tech wave in Q3 2022 as Ukraine-driven NATO budget pledges drove defense stocks to dramatically outperform a bear market. BOTZ adds broad robotics/industrial-automation exposure at what looks like an early buildup price (tech selloff in 2022 compressed valuations), anchored by Fanuc's July 2022 campus doubling as a concrete demand signal. Nuclear (NuScale/SMR) and quantum (QTUM) are not yet added due to the 3-add-per-period cap; both are candidates for the next rebalance. No removes are warranted: NVDA's August 2022 gaming-segment miss is a crypto-cycle headwind, not an AI data-center thesis breach, and the remaining holdings all retain their wave rationale.
